signals: Observed behavior linked to Active Scanning (T1595).

Threat intel context: External infrastructure performs broad service enumeration against internet-facing assets to identify reachable attack surface and protocol behavior. Common tooling observed or simulated: nmap, masscan, zmap.
Observed behavior linked to Scanning IP Blocks (T1595.001).
Threat intel context: Distributed probes sweep known netblocks to map live hosts and open management ports before targeting. Common tooling observed or simulated: masscan, nmap, zgrab.
Observed behavior linked to Vulnerability Scanning (T1595.002).
Threat intel context: Automated scans profile software versions and check for known CVEs on exposed services. Common tooling observed or simulated: nuclei, nessus, openvas.
Observed behavior linked to Wordlist Scanning (T1595.003).
Threat intel context: HTTP endpoints are brute-enumerated with common paths and filenames to discover hidden admin or backup content. Common tooling observed or simulated: ffuf, gobuster, dirsearch.
Observed behavior linked to Gather Victim Network Information (T1590).

Threat intel context: Adversary profiling consolidates victim network metadata to prioritize high-value external and trust-connected assets. Common tooling observed or simulated: shodan, censys, securitytrails.
Observed behavior linked to Domain Properties (T1590.001).

Threat intel context: Registrar and DNS records are reviewed to identify domain ownership, nameservers, and operational footprint. Common tooling observed or simulated: whois, securitytrails, dnstwist.
Observed behavior linked to DNS (T1590.002).
Threat intel context: DNS records are enumerated to identify mail gateways, subdomains, and external service dependencies. Common tooling observed or simulated: dig, nslookup, dnsrecon.
Observed behavior linked to Network Trust Dependencies (T1590.003).
Threat intel context: Third-party network dependencies and federated links are mapped to find indirect access opportunities. Common tooling observed or simulated: amass, crtsh, maltego.
Observed behavior linked to Network Topology (T1590.004).
Threat intel context: Routing paths and exposed service clusters are analyzed to infer segmentation and edge architecture. Common tooling observed or simulated: traceroute, mtr, nmap.
Observed behavior linked to IP Addresses (T1590.005).
Threat intel context: Public IPv4/IPv6 assets are cataloged and correlated with historical ownership and hosting providers. Common tooling observed or simulated: bgpview, whois, shodan.
Observed behavior linked to Network Security Appliances (T1590.006).
Threat intel context: Edge appliance banners and TLS fingerprints reveal firewall, VPN, and WAF technologies in use. Common tooling observed or simulated: nmap, whatwaf, sslyze.
Observed behavior linked to Gather Victim Identity Information (T1589).
Threat intel context: Public identity artifacts are collected to build targeting lists and social engineering pretexts. Common tooling observed or simulated: theHarvester, hunter, linkedin.
Observed behavior linked to Credentials (T1589.001).

Threat intel context: Leaked credential references are searched across breach datasets and paste sources for account reuse opportunities. Common tooling observed or simulated: dehashed, haveibeenpwned, intelx.
Observed behavior linked to Email Addresses (T1589.002).
Threat intel context: Corporate email patterns are enumerated from public sources to build validated recipient lists. Common tooling observed or simulated: theHarvester, hunter, email-format.
Observed behavior linked to Employee Names (T1589.003).
Threat intel context: Personnel names and role context are collected from public profiles and conference material. Common tooling observed or simulated: linkedin, maltego, spiderfoot.
Observed behavior linked to Gather Victim Org Information (T1591).

Threat intel context: Organization-level intelligence is assembled to understand business structure, priorities, and partner exposure. Common tooling observed or simulated: opencorporates, crunchbase, sec-filings.
Observed behavior linked to Determine Physical Locations (T1591.001).

Threat intel context: Office, datacenter, and facility location references are gathered to support geo-focused targeting. Common tooling observed or simulated: google-maps, openstreetmap, company-sites.
Observed behavior linked to Business Relationships (T1591.002).
Threat intel context: Supplier, MSP, and partner relationships are mapped to identify supply-chain pathways. Common tooling observed or simulated: open-source-news, sec-filings, vendor-portals.
Observed behavior linked to Identify Business Tempo (T1591.003).

Threat intel context: Operational cycles such as quarter close, release windows, and support shifts are profiled for timing attacks. Common tooling observed or simulated: investor-relations, status-pages, social-media.
Observed behavior linked to Identify Roles (T1591.004).
Threat intel context: Key personnel roles are identified to target privileged users and decision-makers. Common tooling observed or simulated: linkedin, org-charting, company-blog.
Observed behavior linked to Gather Victim Host Information (T1592).

Threat intel context: Host-level technology indicators are profiled to estimate endpoint, server, and user environment characteristics. Common tooling observed or simulated: shodan, censys, wappalyzer.
Observed behavior linked to Hardware (T1592.001).
Threat intel context: Publicly exposed banners and procurement traces are used to infer hardware families and infrastructure tiers. Common tooling observed or simulated: shodan, nmap, snmpwalk.
Observed behavior linked to Software (T1592.002).
Threat intel context: Web and network fingerprints identify application stacks, versions, and likely patch levels. Common tooling observed or simulated: whatweb, wappalyzer, nuclei.
Observed behavior linked to Firmware (T1592.003).
Threat intel context: Device interfaces are queried for firmware versions to estimate exploitability and lifecycle status. Common tooling observed or simulated: nmap, router-scan, upnp-tools.
Observed behavior linked to Client Configurations (T1592.004).

Threat intel context: Published docs and telemetry clues reveal endpoint defaults, VPN usage, and client-side security controls. Common tooling observed or simulated: support-docs, browser-fingerprinting, mdm-artifacts.
Observed behavior linked to Search Open Websites/Domains (T1593).
Threat intel context: Open web assets are searched for exposed documents, scripts, and operational references. Common tooling observed or simulated: google-dorking, bing, duckduckgo.
Observed behavior linked to Social Media (T1593.001).
Threat intel context: Social posts are mined for technology clues, employee movement, and campaign-specific language. Common tooling observed or simulated: x-search, linkedin, osintgram.
Observed behavior linked to Search Engines (T1593.002).
Threat intel context: General search indexing is used to discover unintended exposures and forgotten subdomains. Common tooling observed or simulated: google, bing, yandex.
Observed behavior linked to Code Repositories (T1593.003).
Threat intel context: Public repositories are scanned for secrets, internal URLs, and deployment metadata. Common tooling observed or simulated: github, gitlab, trufflehog.
Observed behavior linked to Search Victim-Owned Websites (T1594).
Threat intel context: Victim-owned portals are crawled for metadata leaks, admin paths, and contact structures. Common tooling observed or simulated: hakrawler, waybackurls, gau.
Observed behavior linked to Search Open Technical Databases (T1596).
Threat intel context: Technical intelligence databases are queried to enrich external exposure and infrastructure posture. Common tooling observed or simulated: shodan, censys, crtsh.
Observed behavior linked to DNS/Passive DNS (T1596.001).
Threat intel context: Passive DNS history is reviewed to enumerate legacy infrastructure and pivot on historical records. Common tooling observed or simulated: passivetotal, securitytrails, dnsdb.
Observed behavior linked to WHOIS (T1596.002).
Threat intel context: WHOIS data is queried for registrant patterns, registrar changes, and domain administration links. Common tooling observed or simulated: whois, rdap, securitytrails.
Observed behavior linked to Digital Certificates (T1596.003).
Threat intel context: Certificate transparency and SAN fields are mined to discover hidden or staging subdomains. Common tooling observed or simulated: crtsh, certstream, openssl.
Observed behavior linked to CDNs (T1596.004).
Threat intel context: CDN mappings are analyzed to identify origin exposure risks and cache-bypass opportunities. Common tooling observed or simulated: dig, cdnfinder, securitytrails.
Observed behavior linked to Scan Databases (T1596.005).
Threat intel context: Specialized data services are searched for prior scans, breach traces, and infrastructure metadata. Common tooling observed or simulated: shodan, fofa, zoomeye.
Observed behavior linked to Search Closed Sources (T1597).

Threat intel context: Restricted or paid sources are consulted to obtain intelligence unavailable on open channels. Common tooling observed or simulated: commercial-intel-feeds, industry-portals, closed-forums.
Observed behavior linked to Threat Intel Vendors (T1597.001).

Threat intel context: Commercial threat intelligence reports are reviewed for victim-specific infrastructure and actor overlap. Common tooling observed or simulated: recorded-future, mandiant, crowdstrike.
Observed behavior linked to Purchase Technical Data (T1597.002).

Threat intel context: Technical datasets are acquired to accelerate targeting with high-confidence infrastructure context. Common tooling observed or simulated: data-brokers, intel-marketplaces, enrichment-apis.
Observed behavior linked to Phishing for Information (T1598).

Threat intel context: Pretext contact attempts are designed to elicit org details, technical workflows, or access procedures. Common tooling observed or simulated: custom-mailer, social-engineering-framework, voip.
Observed behavior linked to Spearphishing Service (T1598.001).
Threat intel context: Messaging or collaboration services are used to request sensitive internal process information. Common tooling observed or simulated: slack, teams, telegram.
Observed behavior linked to Spearphishing Attachment (T1598.002).
Threat intel context: Attachments are used as lures to solicit user feedback and gather environment-specific details. Common tooling observed or simulated: office-docs, pdf-lures, mail-templates.
Observed behavior linked to Spearphishing Link (T1598.003).
Threat intel context: Targeted links collect interaction telemetry and reveal identity or environment attributes. Common tooling observed or simulated: short-links, tracking-redirects, phishing-kits.
Observed behavior linked to Spearphishing Voice (T1598.004).
Threat intel context: Voice calls are used to gather support processes, contact routing, and verification methods. Common tooling observed or simulated: voip, caller-id-spoofing, call-scripts.
Observed behavior linked to Search Threat Vendor Data (T1681).
Threat intel context: Public and partner threat vendor telemetry is queried for indicators tied to victim infrastructure. Common tooling observed or simulated: virus-total, abuseipdb, otx.
